title: Attention Is All You Need
audience: ML students who know vectors and softmax but are new to attention.
central_question: How does a Transformer replace recurrence with attention and positional information?

teaching_units.name: Scaled Dot-Product Attention
teaching_units.goal: Show how query-key similarities become weights over value vectors.
teaching_units.visual_example: A query token asks which context tokens matter; a bar chart of weights mixes value cards.

teaching_units.name: Multi-Head Attention
teaching_units.goal: Explain why multiple attention heads can attend to different relations.
teaching_units.visual_example: Three colored heads attend to syntax, position, and object relation, then concatenate.

teaching_units.name: Sinusoidal Positional Encoding
teaching_units.goal: Show why a non-recurrent model needs position signals.
teaching_units.visual_example: Token embeddings start unordered; sinusoidal waves add distinguishable position stripes.

comparison_axes: no position signal vs sinusoidal position signal
